response.justification: Candidate brings rare geospatial domain expertise and senior-level engineering maturity, making them valuable for routing infrastructure work. However, modern C++ expertise is dated (last professional work 2014-2015 at National Instruments); primary recent skills are JavaScript/TypeScript and web/full-stack development rather than systems-level or performance-critical infrastructure. While the role values willingness to expand expertise and the candidate has demonstrated this across multiple languages, rebuilding C++ proficiency for production routing pipelines represents a material ramp-up challenge that may outweigh domain knowledge advantages.
strengths: Deep geospatial and mapping domain expertise (GISCollective founder, Green Map work) that is rare and directly applicable to routing systems
Senior-level engineer with proven track record designing and operating distributed systems at scale, including microservices infrastructure handling millions of requests per second
Strong problem-solving mindset grounded in testing, TDD, and demonstrated comfort learning across diverse technical domains
concerns: Modern C++ expertise is significantly dated—last professional work in 2014-2015; primary recent skills focus on JavaScript, TypeScript, and web/full-stack technologies
Career trajectory has been web and full-stack development rather than systems-level or performance-critical infrastructure engineering
No demonstrated experience with routing tile generation, data pipeline optimization, real-time data processing, or navigation infrastructure at scale

key_factors.factor: Web-focused background vs. systems engineering needs
